question: Will Donald Trump post 100-119 Truth Social posts from April 3 to April 10, 2026?

fragility_points: A tracker revision below 100 or above 119 would materially weaken the view.
Evidence of many excluded replies being counted would reduce confidence.
key_assumptions: XTracker's 103 total correctly applies the contract closely enough that any residual error will not move the result out of the 100-119 bucket.
No late tracker correction or sync failure changes the total before resolution.
main_logical_chain: Start from the outside view that Trump often posts at very high weekly volume on Truth Social.
Check the market's named resolution source rather than relying on narrative priors alone.
The exact XTracker market window returns a total of 103, which lies inside 100-119.
Residual uncertainty is mostly about tracker rule implementation, not about whether the posting pace is behaviorally plausible.
payload.main_thesis: The contract's named tracker currently shows 103 Trump Truth Social posts in the exact window, putting the 100-119 bucket in-range unless there is a material rule-implementation error.
quote_anchors: governing source of truth is XTracker's Post Counter
payload.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is strong because XTracker is the named resolution surface, but evidence independence is only medium-low and auditability around replies/deleted posts is not perfect.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract excludes replies unless they appear on the main feed, and I did not manually classify all 103 records one by one.
Deleted-post treatment and tracker implementation details limit truly independent recounting.
strongest_supports: XTracker tracking stats for the exact market window returned total = 103.
The tracked user is verified realDonaldTrump on TRUTH_SOCIAL and matches the public Truth Social identity.
Trump's posting frequency is high enough on base rates that 100-119 is not an exotic outcome.
payload.timing_relevance: Immediate; the market resolves at noon ET on 2026-04-10 and the tracker was checked shortly before completion with the total already at 103.
unresolved_ambiguities: Public API output reviewed did not clearly expose a reply/main-feed classification field for every returned post.
I did not complete a full independent manual recount from exported tracker data.
payload.what_would_change_view: A tracker correction, confirmed reply overcount, or proof that enough valid posts were missed/miscoded to move the count outside 100-119.
